sections.heading: The Five Basic Sentence Patterns
sections.body:
1. SV: "She sleeps." (intransitive — no object)
2. SVO: "He reads books." (transitive — one object)
3. SVOO: "She gave him a gift." (ditransitive — two objects)
4. SVC: "He is a teacher." (linking verb + complement)
5. SVOC: "We call him Ali." (object + complement)

Pattern 3 details:
• She gave (V) him (IO) a book (DO).
• IO = indirect person, DO = direct thing
• Also: "She gave a book to him" (preposition version)

Pattern 5 details:
• We call (V) him (O) Ali (OC).
• Ali = what him becomes/is called

sections.heading: Adjective and Adverb Placement
sections.body:
Adjectives BEFORE nouns:
• "a beautiful house" (not "a house beautiful")
• Multiple adjectives: opinion-size-age-shape-color
  "A beautiful large old round red ball"

Adverbs of manner AFTER the verb:
• "She speaks English fluently"
• "He drives carefully"
• For short verbs, can go before: "She quickly ran"

Adverbs of frequency BEFORE the main verb:
• "I always wake up early"
• "She never eats meat"
• But AFTER "be": "He is always late"

sections.heading: Sentence Types
sections.body:
1. Declarative: statement (period)
"English is a useful language."

2. Interrogative: question (question mark)
"Is English a useful language?"
"What language do you speak?"

3. Imperative: command (period)
"Open the door." "Please sit down."

4. Exclamatory: strong emotion (exclamation)
"What a beautiful day!" "How amazing!"

Question formation:
• Yes/no: Move the auxiliary verb before the subject
  "She can swim" → "Can she swim?"
• WH- questions: Add question word at the start
  "She speaks French" → "What does she speak?"

sections.heading: Practice Sentences
sections.body:
Write complete sentences using these words:
1. cat / sleep / roof
2. children / play / garden
3. she / study / English / university
4. they / not / understand / lesson
5. when / train / arrive?

Answers:
1. The cat sleeps on the roof.
2. The children play in the garden.
3. She studies English at university.
4. They don't understand the lesson.
5. When does the train arrive?

summary: Master the 5 sentence patterns and word order rules. English is strict about word order — unlike Hindi, you can't rearrange freely. Practice writing 5 sentences daily.
